question: Explain the main components of NPM.

nuscale_ch05_0006.title: System Evaluation
nuscale_ch05_0006.summary: This section describes the evaluation of the NuScale Power Plant’s system performance, focusing on optimizing steady-state parameters such as coolant temperatures, flow rates, and pressure to maximize electrical generation and maintain safety margins. Calculations, verified by hand and using NRELAP5 software, account for uncertainties and ensure low pressure losses to support the plant’s natural circulation design. The analysis also confirms that certain uncertainties, like SG tube plugging, do not affect primary coolant flow rate determination.

nuscale_ch05_0002.title: Summary Description
nuscale_ch05_0002.summary: The NuScale reactor coolant system (RCS) uses natural circulation without pumps or external piping to transfer heat from the reactor core to the steam generator, ensuring safe and efficient operation. The RCS design supports up to twelve independent modules, maintains water chemistry, and ensures uniform boron concentration to protect fuel integrity during all operating conditions.

nuscale_ch05_0008.title: Compliance with Codes and Code Cases
nuscale_ch05_0008.summary: This section describes how the NuScale Power Module (NPM) and its reactor coolant pressure boundary (RCPB) components comply with relevant U.S. regulations, specifically 10 CFR 50.55a and associated General Design Criteria. It details that these components are designed, fabricated, and tested to ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code (BPVC) Section III, 2013 Edition standards, and clarifies the classification and quality assurance requirements for various piping and systems connected to the reactor coolant system.

nuscale_ch05_0005.title: System Components
nuscale_ch05_0005.summary: This section describes the main components of the NuScale reactor system, focusing on the Reactor Pressure Vessel (RPV), Reactor Coolant System (RCS) piping, and the Pressurizer (PZR). The RPV serves as a primary barrier to fission product release and houses key reactor internals, while the RCS piping connects various system elements and the PZR is integrated within the RPV to help control system pressure. Each component's functions and connections are briefly outlined, with references to more detailed sections.

nuscale_ch05_0004.title: System Description
nuscale_ch05_0004.summary: The Reactor Coolant System (RCS) in the NuScale design is located inside the containment vessel and includes components such as the reactor pressure vessel, pressurizer, and associated piping and controls. It removes heat from the reactor core to the steam generators using natural circulation, and features systems for removing noncondensible gases and maintaining pressure boundary integrity to prevent radionuclide release. The RCS interfaces with several other systems, including emergency core cooling and chemical control systems.

nuscale_ch05_0021.title: Reactor Coolant System Piping
nuscale_ch05_0021.summary: The section discusses the design and degradation assessment of the NuScale reactor coolant system's steam generator (SG) tubing. It notes that the main degradation observed in similar operating reactors is wear from flow-induced vibration or foreign objects, and that NuScale's design—with its helical tubing and novel support structure—does not increase risk compared to existing designs. Prototypic testing validates the new support design, and typical SG program guidelines remain appropriate, with wear identified as the most likely degradation mechanism and corrosion risks assessed as low.

edges.description: Section B provides a general summary of the main components of the NPM, while Section A references and builds upon this by evaluating system performance and referring to further component descriptions in Section B.

Section A describes the system evaluation and operational criteria for the NPM's main components, while Section B specifies that these components must comply with regulatory codes and standards, connecting the design and performance details in A to the regulatory requirements in B.
Section B lists specific system components and operational steps that are referenced and evaluated in Section A's discussion of system performance and optimization criteria.

Section A evaluates the performance criteria and operational parameters of the NuScale Power Module (NPM) systems, while Section B provides a detailed description of the main components and functions of those systems, connecting the evaluation in A to the specific system features in B.

Section A references Section B by directing the reader to Section 5.4 for further description of the steam generators (SGs), which are a main component of the NPM, thereby connecting the general system evaluation to the detailed component and subsystem design.

Section A identifies the main components of the NPM, including the reactor pressure vessel and integral pressurizer, while Section B specifies that these components comply with regulatory codes and standards, connecting the described components to their required regulatory integrity.

Section A provides a general overview of the NPM's main components, while Section B details specific operational procedures and components (such as the PZR, SGs, and control systems) that are part of the NPM, thereby specifying the general description in Section A.

Section A provides a general overview of the Reactor Coolant System (RCS) as a main component of the NPM, while Section B specifies detailed features and functions of the RCS mentioned in Section A, such as degasification and pressure boundary integrity.

Section A provides a general overview of the main components of the NPM, specifically mentioning the reactor coolant system (RCS), while Section B gives detailed information about a specific component (SG tubing) within the RCS, thus Section B specifies details for the general description in Section A.

Section A establishes that each NPM complies with regulatory codes for design and construction, while Section B describes the specific system components and operational steps of the NPM, thus Section B provides details/specifications for the general compliance described in Section A.

Section A states that each NPM complies with specific regulatory codes for design and construction, while Section B describes the physical system components and their functions, showing that the described components are those that must comply with the codes referenced in Section A.

Section A establishes that the NPMs comply with regulatory codes for reactor coolant boundary integrity, while Section B discusses specific design features and operational experience of the NPM's reactor coolant system piping, providing supporting evidence that the NPM's design aligns with established safety practices.

Section A describes operational steps involving the PZR water level and degasification, while Section B provides the system-level function of the degasification line and RVVs, thus Section A specifies operational details for the general system description in Section B.

Section A outlines the operational procedures and components involved in the NPM system, while Section B provides specific design details and considerations for one of those components—the steam generators (SGs)—thus Section B specifies details for a component generally described in Section A.

Section A provides a general system-level description of the Reactor Coolant System (RCS) and its functions, while Section B specifies detailed design considerations and operational experience for a key RCS component (the steam generator tubing), thus Section B provides specific details that elaborate on the general description in Section A.
